mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of triggered instances or unemployment spikes fail to predict return direction with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which amplifies corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts yield negative forward returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production acceleration fails to predict short-term returns with Rank IC below 0.01 or yields negative returns during tightening phases.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings above 0.35% force central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, increasing discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation months produce positive forward returns in more than 60% of cases or inflation threshold shows no predictive power with t-stat below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of payroll growth and unemployment decline yields negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly correlates with improved corporate profit margins and economic momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth levels show zero correlation with 5-day forward returns with Rank IC below 0.005 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in the index of industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction and weakening supply-side momentum, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Consecutive industrial production declines are followed by positive forward returns in more than 65% of instances.
